title: The Future of Forests
content: Human communities have benefitted immensely from trees, but tree communities (i.e., forests) haven’t always fared so well in the bargain. What can we do differently to ensure a forested future?
creator: Richard Heinberg
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Human Nature Odyssey: Episode 20: Joanna Macy & The Great Turning — with Jess Serrante
content: Legendary activist Joanna Macy called this moment the Great Unraveling—a time when our ecological, political, economic, and social systems crumble. And yet, she also insisted that we stand on the threshold of a Great Turning: a profound transition toward a more just and sustainable world.
creator: Alex Leff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Could the West Lose the Resource Wars? AI, Rare Earths, and Economic Statecraft
content: In this episode, Nate is joined by financial and economic analysts, Craig Tindale and Michael Every, to discuss the widespread implications of growing geopolitical tensions over scarce resources and the rapidly changing foreign policy and economic statecraft that countries are implementing in response.
creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Water and Security in the Middle East: Lessons from the Iranian Crisis
content: Water, through its progressive scarcity, is redrawing the map of vulnerabilities and powers. Countries that make its management a factor of internal cohesion and regional cooperation will be better equipped for the decades to come.
creator: El Habib Ben Amara
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Ravmed: Where the Mountains Guard the Seeds of Life
content: Ravmed’s story is not just about wheat. It is about people who refused to let their heritage disappear, who safeguarded what their ancestors handed down, and who continue—season by season—to plant a future rooted firmly in the past.
creator: Umed Qurbonbekov
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Escaping the Machine
content: I want to stress that you will enjoy everything about localizing your life. You will be happier and healthier. You will have more time for the things that are important to you. After an initial investment in some things, your life will be less costly. You will need less income. You will take pride in the work that you do and in the community that you help build. And you will have that community.
creator: Eliza Daley
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: A response to Jonathan Aldred: On the tortuous relationship between GDP and macroecological footprints
content: Is decoupling happening, yes, or no? And if not, could it ever happen? Over the course of a few weeks, The Guardian published several pieces on the topic that may appear contradictory, arguing both that “economic growth [is] no longer linked to carbon emissions” and that “economic growth is still heating up the planet.”
creator: Timothée Parrique
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Ecosphere Lessons
content: The hunter-gatherer knows instinctively in their bones that separating oneself from ancient ecology is bonkers. Listen to them.
creator: Tom Murphy
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Britain’s Political Eruption
content: And you don’t have to be much of a political strategist to work out that voters are going to punish a social democratic party for not looking after the health sector, or for a weak economy—one a core trusted issue, the other a basic test of government competence—more than they will for migration numbers that are misunderstood and repeatedly misrepresented.
creator: Andrew Curry
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Ultra-Processed Information: AI and the Coming Deluge of Noise
content: In this week’s Frankly, Nate explores the growing sense that many people feel disoriented and overwhelmed in a world increasingly saturated with digital content.
creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The Future is Local: Mitmach-Region Vorarlberg
content: For centuries, social life in Europe was radically organized at the local level. Language, food, work, belonging, and identity were closely tied to specific places: landscapes, villages, and markets. Relationships were manageable and resilient. Place was not a backdrop — it was the system.
creator: Caspar Scharmer
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: NOW! IT’S TIME
content: There are simpler, healthier paths we can take. With instability in more than our climate systems, it is time to examine where we live, where we draw the line.
creator: Zia Gallina
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The Cathedral of Plastic: How We Manufactured a Year of Excess
content: We need our autonomy back. And that starts with walking out of the toy store empty-handed and realizing that the most resilient thing we can give the next generation isn't a piece of plastic - it's a planet that isn't a graveyard for their old toys.
creator: Saskia Karges
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.
